HERRERA, CITY ATTORNEY FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO SERVED MAY-14-2010, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT BURKE, EILEEN

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS' ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. CHAU LISTED THE FOLSOM STREET PROPERTY AT $1.39 MILLION AND HAS ADMITTED THE VALUE. THERE WAS NO TRADE OF REASONABLY EQUIVALENT VALUE. PLAINTIFF ESTABLISHES LIABILITY BECAUSE OF AN ACTUAL INTENT TO HINDER BASED ON BADGES OF FRAUD. (CC SEC. 3439.04(B).) THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES AND HARMS ARE IN PLAINTIFF'S FAVOR. CHAU HAS BEEN THWARTING THE COLLECTION PROCESS FOR YEARS AND HAS BEEN HELD IN CONTEMPT. BURKE ACCEPTED THE PROPERTY WITH KNOWLEDGE OF THE PENDING SUITS. COURT ADOPTS THE LANGUAGE OF THE PROPOSED PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, EXCEPT PAGE 3, LINE 18, MODIFY "SHALL" TO "MAY" BE LIABLE. ORDER RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.(302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EILEEN BURKE MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT DOES NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT LEGAL REASON UNDER CCP SECTION 473 TO WARRANT RELIEF FROM DEFAULT. THE PRESS OF BUSINESS ALONE DOES NOT CONSTITUTE EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. SEE HUH VS. WANG (2008) 158 CAL.APP. 4TH 1406, 1424. DEFENDANT DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE DILIGENCE IN ATTEMPTING TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT IN VIEW OF THE UNEXPLAINED DELAY IN FILING THIS MOTION AFTER THE FAILURE TO ACHIEVE A STIPULATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar May-27-2010 Off Calendar

Order To Show Cause Re: Prelimianry Injunction
Off Calendar May-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiffs' Order to Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Michael S. Weiss, Esq. Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3824 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Eileen Burke, Esq. of Burke Law Offices (415) 578-3925 appearing as attorney in pro per for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Preliminary injunction granted. Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the merits. Chau listed the Folsom Street property at $1.39 million and has admitted the value. There was no trade of reasonably equivalent value. Plaintiff establishes liability because of an actual intent to hinder based on badges of fraud. (CC sec. 3439.04(b).) The balance of equities and harms are in plaintiff's favor. Chau has been thwarting the collection process for years and has been held in contempt. Burke accepted the property with knowledge of the pending suits. Court adopts the language of the proposed preliminary injunction, except page 3, line 18, modify "shall" to "may" be liable. Order re: preliminary injunction signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JUDY CHAU, WILLIAM CHAU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to answer. Stipulated facts from prior action do not contradict instant complaint. Defendants lack standing to raise CC section 1714.10. Taking the allegations as true, CCSF states a cause of action for fraudulent transfer and conspiracy. (Counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EILEEN BURKE Motion for Relief from Default. Appearances: Michael S. Weiss, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3824 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Eileen Burke, Attorney at Law, (415) 578-3925 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied without prejudice. Defendant does not provide sufficient legal reason under CCP section 473 to warrant relief from default. The press of business alone does not constitute excusable neglect. See Huh vs. Wang (2008) 158 Cal.App. 4th 1406, 1424. Defendant does not demonstrate diligence in attempting to set aside the default in view of the unexplained delay in filing this motion after the failure to achieve a stipulation. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
